© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Key Takeaways:
• An Evergreen product launch is how you take a one-time product launch and turn
it into an ongoing, process.
• New leads will get a product launch shortly after they opt in and come into your
world. Even a simple launch sequence will kill any “hope marketing”.
• Here’s one way to do it: perform a product launch, put together a sequence, and
then leave it on the market. Another way: do a launch, pull the product off the
market, and then relaunch it every so often.
• Or you can do Evergreen - all new leads getting a product launch. An important
thing to note is that there’s no one right thing for everyone. You can have multiple
products with multiple different strategies - it doesn't need to line up perfectly.
• You have to think about how the product is best delivered - this will help you
decide the strategy. Through my experience I found that it was very effective to
hold an “online class” and launch every so often.
• Another consideration is how you’ll drive traffic. A downfall of one-time or once a
year product launches is that it becomes more difficult to buy traffic.
• If you’re buying traffic and the prospect has to wait a long time for the relaunch,
you don’t know if those leads will convert anymore down the line.
• With an evergreen, the lead will go through your PLC’s just 3 days after joining
your list. Now you’re sending traffic in and within 10-15 days you find out whether
it converts.
• Another caveat: if you’re trying to do an Evergreen Launch with JV’s, it can be
tougher to motivate partners to promote. Their calendars are typically full with JV
Launches.
• Where your strategy lies will depend on your lead streams. Evergreen works well
when you’re buying traffic, but you can use both techniques and we’ll get to that
next.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
What exactly is an Evergreen Product Launch?
It’s the way you take the power of your one-time product launch and you turn it
into an ongoing process.
So new leads come into your business, no matter how they come in those new
leads they’re going to get a product launch, they’re going to get the whole sequence
shortly after they come into your world, after they learn about you, after they opt in
really is what we’re talking about.
You basically have this sequence and here’s Prelaunch Content, Prelaunch
Content, Prelaunch Content, and sales letter. Basically no matter when the leads come in
shortly after they opt in they’re going to get the first piece of Prelaunch Content, then
they’re going to get the second piece, the third piece, then they’re going to get the sales
letter. So it’s basically your launch sequence, but instead of just being a onetime episodic
event it becomes something that no matter when someone comes into your world they
are going to get your product launch.
Let’s look at this big picture.
There are lots of different ways to bring your product to market. One of them is
the good old “hope marketing.” I talked about that way back in the beginning, that hope
marketing where you create something, you put it on the market and you hope it does
well.
Since you’ve come so far in Product Launch Formula I assume I’ve totally beat
the hope marketing out of you and you’re not going to bring something into the market
without doing some type of a launch sequence, because even a very simple launch
sequence is always going to kill hope marketing.
Another way you can do it is you can just basically do a product launch, but do
the whole sequence, do a product launch, put it on the market, and then it’s always on
the market. That’s a perfectly fine way to do it. I’ve done that many times in the past and
I’m sure I’ll do it more times in the future. You basically put together your sequence, use
that power to get the word out and to grab instant momentum and then you just let it
run and let it roll along.
Another way you can do it is you can do a launch and then you can pull the
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
product off the market and then do occasional relaunches. That’s really what I’ve been
doing with Product Launch Formula for the last few years. It’s been working fantastic,
just gangbusters. I’ll talk a little bit about that a little later on, some of the
considerations. But, basically it’s worked very well.
Or you could do evergreen, so new leads coming into your business basically get a
product launch.
One important thing here, there’s no right answer for all situations. We’ll talk
about some of those situations and the considerations in just a minute.
Another thing to remember is you can have multiple products and use multiple
different strategies. So, maybe you have one product that is always on the market, you
have another product that is on the market, you pull it off, then you do another relaunch
every four months, every six months, or nine months or every year. I know a lot of people
that do that, they have a product that they roll out every single year. Then maybe you
have another product that’s on an Evergreen Launch, so someone comes into your world
and a week later they get a launch sequence.
You can mix and match these strategies, you don’t have to use just one strategy.
And it can be a really great thing in your business to mix those.
So, when you’re thinking about which of these to do you need to think about your
underlying strategy. One important thing is how is the product best delivered? Like with
Product Launch Formula what I’ve found is way back in the old days with the very first
version I did a launch for about a week and then after we did that launch it stayed on the
market for like two and a half years, let’s just say two years. It was on the market and
sold it every single day.
That worked great, except for then when I did 2.0 I decided to go to this method
where I did a launch, pulled it back off the market and ran it as an online class and we all
went through the class together instead of just shipping out a box of stuff. Low and
behold I found people got much better results when we ran it as a class and we all went
through the material together and we did those Q&A calls.
So, in the case of Product Launch Formula I found out that it was really effective
to do those periodic launches and then run things as an ongoing class as opposed to
having it on the market all the time where I just shipped people a box of CDs and DVDs.
So the product delivery is one thing of what your strategy should be, whether it should be
evergreen or one-time launch or periodic relaunches.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Another big thing is how you’re going to drive traffic. One of the downfalls of
doing a one-time product launch or doing the every six or nine-month product launch is
it’s tougher to buy traffic, because if you’re buying traffic here’s your squeeze page, and
then you’ve got all these different ways of buying traffic and you’re sending people onto a
squeeze page. And that’s awesome. You might be buying Adwords and Facebook and
whatever. You send them into a squeeze page, after they get to the squeeze page then
where do they go?
So if they just go into the squeeze page and then they have to wait six or nine
months for a relaunch, if they have to wait all this time for a relaunch, you’re spending
money here and you don’t know – well, first of all you’re just spending money and you’re
not going to get any money back for a long time, that’s one bad thing. The other bad
thing is you just don’t know if these are good lead sources that are going to convert.
These two lead sources might crush it, these guys might convert really well when
you get down to doing your launch and maybe these don’t, they do really poorly. So you
can’t be spending money for six or nine months hoping that traffic is going to convert,
not knowing.
But if you’ve gone evergreen, let’s go back, now you’ve got these lead sources and
you’re sending traffic in here and then three days after they join your list they get
Prelaunch Content piece number one, then number two, then number three, then they go
to your sales letter. Now it’s basically you’re sending traffic in and within maybe,
whatever, 10 to 15 days you’re finding out whether it converts. Now you can spend that
on traffic.
Part of this is based on lead sources and lead streams. If you’re just purely 100%
JV traffic this can vary, but usually JV traffic it’s easier to motivate JVs to get onboard
for a big product launch, because in most markets there’s a lot of product launches going
on and people are trying to get the JVs on board to promote.
Let’s go back here and do a quick illustration here. The best way to do this is like
this, let’s pretend that’s a calendar even though there’s more days in a week than that.
There’s one product launch going on here, that’s his prelaunch and this is his open cart,
then there’s another prelaunch that starts here and it goes through here and then they
open the cart. Here’s big JV #1, here’s JV #2, and here’s JV #3.
These guys are all committed to these launches, they’re committed to all these
launches and now you come along and say, “I got this evergreen thing, do you want to
promote for me?” They look at their calendar and go, “Well, there’s no room here, so I’ll
put you in next month. Let’s talk about it next month.” Then next month comes and that
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 6 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
calendar looks just like this yet again and they’re like, “How about next month?” Then
it’s once again the calendar is all full.
There are so many promotions going on that each of these launches, if they want
to participate in those launches they have to participate when they are on the calendar.
Your Evergreen Launch is easy to put off, people will keep saying, “We’ll get to you.” The
reality is with evergreen sometimes, not always and not in all markets, but sometimes it
can be tougher to motivate people because you don’t have this big date circled.
It’s not like you’re out there and you’re saying, “Hey, my prelaunch is on June 1st
and I really need you there on June 1st, and then the prelaunch ends June 10th and I
really need you to hit cart open on June 11th, and it goes until the close on June 15th.”
You’re telling them these dates in April and you’re saying, “I really need you there. Can I
count on you? Circle those dates.” That’s what happens in a product launch and that’s
how you get partners to get on board and all come through for you, whereas with
evergreen it can be a little bit harder because it’s easier to put you off. They’re like, “I’ve
got something going on, I’ll get you next month.”
So where your strategy is going to lie sort of depends on your lead streams and
there’s a lot of different strategic considerations, and those were a couple of them. When
you’re buying traffic evergreen really works well. When you’re completely dependent on
JV traffic then it’s more the periodic launches will often work better. The deal is you can
use both, and we’ll get to that.
